patches.title: Infer Container for Missing Object
patches.trigger: Target object is not listed in the initial observation and not in inventory, but the task implies it exists
action_flow: Check inventory for the target object
If not present, identify candidate containers (cabinets, drawers, fridge, sink, countertops) based on common storage priors
Visit and open each container in a logical order (e.g., cabinets first, then drawers) without skipping
Inspect contents until the target object is found
avoid: Repeatedly looking around without opening closed containers
Assuming the object is not present without checking all likely containers
Wasting time on non-container objects like appliances when the object is likely stored
patches.principle: Target object is not listed in the initial observation and not in inventory, but the task implies it exists. Steps: Check inventory for the target object; If not present, identify candidate containers (cabinets, drawers, fridge, sink, countertops) based on common storage priors; Visit and open each container in a logical order (e.g., cabinets first, then drawers) without skipping; Inspect contents until the target object is found
patches.when_to_apply: Target object is not listed in the initial observation and not in inventory, but the task implies it exists

patches.title: Enforce Cleaning Prior to Placement
patches.trigger: Task requires the target object to be clean (e.g., 'clean some X' or 'put clean X in Y')
action_flow: Locate and pick up the target object if not already held
Go to the sink (or designated cleaning area)
Use the sink to clean the object (if admissible action available)
Then proceed to the target receptacle and place the object
avoid: Placing the object without confirming it is clean
Attempting to clean the object after placing it
Searching for cleaning tools other than the sink if it is the only cleaning receptacle
patches.principle: Task requires the target object to be clean (e.g., 'clean some X' or 'put clean X in Y'). Steps: Locate and pick up the target object if not already held; Go to the sink (or designated cleaning area); Use the sink to clean the object (if admissible action available); Then proceed to the target receptacle and place the object
patches.when_to_apply: Task requires the target object to be clean (e.g., 'clean some X' or 'put clean X in Y')

patches.title: Progressive Subgoal Follow-Through
patches.trigger: Task involves multiple sequential subgoals (e.g., find, clean, place) where later subgoals depend on earlier ones
action_flow: Break down the task into ordered subgoals (e.g., locate object, acquire object, transform object, deliver object)
Execute each subgoal completely before moving to the next
If a subgoal cannot be completed, re-evaluate the environment and adjust the plan without skipping steps
avoid: Jumping to a later subgoal (e.g., going to placement location before having the object)
Getting sidetracked by non-target objects that are not part of the subgoal sequence
patches.principle: Task involves multiple sequential subgoals (e.g., find, clean, place) where later subgoals depend on earlier ones. Steps: Break down the task into ordered subgoals (e.g., locate object, acquire object, transform object, deliver object); Execute each subgoal completely before moving to the next; If a subgoal cannot be completed, re-evaluate the environment and adjust the plan without skipping steps
patches.when_to_apply: Task involves multiple sequential subgoals (e.g., find, clean, place) where later subgoals depend on earlier ones
